symbols: dark towering structure bearing the cross — the cross elevated above the world, the axis between earth and heaven
red and purple clouds billowing — the blood of the sacrifice ascending, the cosmic disruption of the crucifixion
golden-yellow sky — the divine glory hidden behind the horror, light persisting through darkness
solitary cross against vast sky — the loneliness of Christ's sacrifice, abandoned by all
description: A surreal vertical composition showing a dark, narrow tower-like structure rising from a dark base into a sky of contrasting golden-yellow and deep red-purple clouds. At the top of the tower, a tiny crucified figure is barely visible against a red oval or disc shape. Billowing clouds of red, purple, and pink surround the tower like smoke from an eruption. The lower portion is dark and undefined. The overall effect is apocalyptic and visionary, with intense warm colors dominating.
theological_description: This surrealist crucifixion transforms Golgotha into a cosmic event. The cross rising from a dark monolith into billowing red clouds evokes the apocalyptic dimensions of Christ's death — when 'the curtain of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, the rocks split' (Matthew 27:51). The red clouds suggest the blood of the Lamb that 'takes away the sin of the world' (John 1:29) ascending to the very heavens. The isolation of the tiny crucified figure against the vast sky captures the full weight of the cry: 'My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?' (Matthew 27:46). The golden sky persisting behind the red horror hints that even in this darkest moment, divine glory has not been extinguished — 'the light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it' (John 1:5). The surreal rendering conveys what no naturalistic painting can: the cross is not merely a historical execution but a rupture in the fabric of reality itself.

scripture_refs.reason: Darkness over the land, the cry of dereliction, and the tearing of the temple veil — the cosmic disruption depicted
The Lamb of God taking away the sin of the world — the red clouds as sacrificial blood
Light shining in darkness — the golden sky persisting behind the horror
